name: GLM-5.1
summary: MIT-licensed open 744B MoE model (40B active, 200K context) — topped SWE-Bench for coding, first open model to beat all closed rivals on real-world code; weights on Hugging Face

name: Claude Managed Agents + ant CLI
summary: public beta for building and deploying Claude agents — sandboxed execution, checkpointing, scoped permissions, SSE streaming; new ant CLI lets you ship agents from the terminal

name: Meta Muse Spark
summary: Meta's first closed-source model — voice/text/image input, live on meta.ai now. Can generate minigames, excels at visual STEM, rolling out to WhatsApp and Ray-Ban glasses

name: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
summary: open-source, production-ready agent framework for Python and .NET — unifies Semantic Kernel + AutoGen with graph-based multi-agent workflows, MCP interop, and a stable LTS API

name: Microsoft MAI Models
summary: three in-house models via Azure Foundry: MAI-Transcribe-1 beats Whisper on 25 languages, MAI-Voice-1 generates 60s audio in under 1s, MAI-Image-2 ranks #3 on Arena image leaderboard

name: Claude Cowork + Dispatch
summary: Claude desktop control now on Windows — clicks, types, opens apps autonomously. Dispatch lets you hand off tasks from your phone while Claude works on your computer

name: Google Gemma 4
summary: four open-weight models (2B–31B) with Apache 2.0 license — runs on a Raspberry Pi or Mac Mini, built from Gemini 3 research, most permissive Gemma release yet

name: Veo 3.1 Lite
summary: accessible text-to-video and image-to-video model — Veo 3.1 Fast pricing slashed to open up developer access for solo creators and small teams

name: GPT-4.1
summary: coding-focused model with 1M token context — GPT-4.1 Mini and Nano also launched; sharper at multi-step instruction following, 26% cheaper than GPT-4o for API calls

name: Llama 4
summary: first multimodal Llama family — Scout (17B/16 experts, 10M context) and Maverick (17B/128 experts) released open-weight; trained on 30T tokens with early fusion of text and image

name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
summary: Google's best reasoning model — experimental release hit #1 on LMArena and Aider coding leaderboards; 1M context, extended thinking, available in AI Studio

name: Gemma 3
summary: open-weight models from 1B to 27B — multimodal (text + image), 128K context, Apache 2.0, runs on a single GPU; 27B variant outperforms Llama 3.3 70B

name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
summary: first hybrid reasoning model — toggleable extended thinking mode, best-in-class coding and agentic tasks; Claude Code CLI launched alongside for terminal-native development

name: GPT-4.5
summary: largest OpenAI model to date — better emotional intelligence, reduced hallucinations, stronger creative and agentic performance; launched in ChatGPT Pro first, then API

name: Grok 3
summary: xAI's flagship reasoning model — trained on 10x the compute of Grok 2, topped math and science benchmarks; DeepSearch and Think modes for multi-step reasoning, free on X

name: o3-mini
summary: cost-efficient reasoning model — three effort levels (low/medium/high), topped AIME math at high effort, available free in ChatGPT; first reasoning model with function calling

name: DeepSeek R1
summary: MIT-licensed open reasoning model matching o1 on math and coding — trained via pure RL with no supervised fine-tuning; distilled 1.5B–70B variants also released; shocked the industry on cost
